title: rubus × sabaudicus
intro: Rubus × sabaudicus, also known as the raspberry rose or sabaudine raspberry, is a hybrid raspberry species obtained by crossing Rubus idaeus and Rubus fruticosus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The leaves, stems, and roots of the Rubus × sabaudicus plant are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rubus × sabaudicus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
